petitioner_background: The petitioner sought classification of the beneficiary as an employment-based immigrant under section 203(b)(1)(A) of the Immigration and Nationality Act, as an alien of extraordinary ability in the arts. No specific details about the beneficiary's field, nationality, or career qualifications are provided in the decision. The petition was filed with the Nebraska Service Center.
outcome_reasoning: The appeal was summarily dismissed pursuant to 8 C.F.R. § 103.3(a)(1)(v) because the petitioner failed to identify specifically any erroneous conclusion of law or statement of fact. Counsel filed a Form I-290B asserting that the director 'erred by misapplying discretionary authority to classify the applicant on EB1 category' and indicated a brief and/or additional evidence would be submitted within 30 days. However, more than 11 months after the appeal was filed, no further documentation had been received by the AAO, so the record was considered complete as presently constituted. Because the petitioner failed to specifically identify any erroneous conclusion of law or statement of fact, the appeal had to be summarily dismissed.
legal_citations: Section 203(b)(1)(A) of the Immigration and Nationality Act
fraud_or_procedural_issues: Procedural dismissal: Counsel filed the Form I-290B indicating a brief and/or additional evidence would be submitted within 30 days of filing the appeal, but more than 11 months later no further documentation had been received. Counsel's only stated ground for appeal was a generalized assertion that the director 'erred by misapplying discretionary authority to classify the applicant on EB1 category,' which failed to specifically identify any erroneous conclusion of law or statement of fact as required by 8 C.F.R. § 103.3(a)(1)(v), resulting in summary dismissal.
